issues.description: Round-1 fix landed. Mechanism is now correctly attributed to RETENTION of persistent, fat-soluble toxins (body cannot metabolize/excrete) accumulating up trophic levels, with an explicit caution: 'do NOT say the 10% energy rule causes magnification; energy loss is just why there's less biomass at the top.' That is exactly the right framing.
issues.suggested_fix: None required. Optional polish: the narration states '~tenfold per step / ~thousandfold cumulative' as if a rule; the slide labels these 'illustrative.' Add one word ('roughly, in this example') to the narration so a student doesn't memorize 10x as a law.

issues.description: Crisp distinction: bioaccumulation = within ONE organism over its lifetime ('over time, one body'); biomagnification = concentration increase BETWEEN trophic levels ('up the chain, many bodies'). Notes both can co-occur. No ambiguity.

Round-1 fix landed. Two clearly separated tracks: THERMAL stress (elevated sea-surface temp) is named the PRIMARY driver of bleaching via expulsion of zooxanthellae; acidification (CO2 + H2O -> H2CO3, lower pH) is a SEPARATE chemistry problem impairing CALCIFICATION. Caution banner reinforces 'don't conflate.' Mechanistically clean and AP-correct.

Round-1 fix landed. The equal-respiration assumption is now stated explicitly ('respiration is assumed to occur at the SAME rate in the light and dark bottles') and the derivation follows from it: dark drop = R alone, so R can be added back to the light NET to recover GPP. GPP = NPP + R is derived, not merely asserted.
issues.suggested_fix: None. Optional: note that this DO method assumes negligible non-photosynthetic O2 exchange (e.g., bacterial photosynthesis / gas diffusion in sealed bottles), but this is beyond CED scope and not needed.

issues.description: Self-consistent and pedagogically sound. Numbers (initial 8, light 10, dark 5) yield NPP +2, R 3, GPP 5, satisfying the GPP > NPP sanity check explicitly reinforced in 14_pause1_silence. Hint formulas match the worked solution.

overall_assessment: All three round-1 flags are genuinely fixed and clearly explained for a student: biomagnification is now driven by toxin retention (not the 10% rule), 09 cleanly separates within-organism bioaccumulation from between-level biomagnification; 10 separates thermal bleaching from CO2-driven acidification/calcification; and 12 states the equal-respiration assumption so GPP = NPP + R is derived. Re-scanning the rest, I find no remaining critical or minor pedagogical traps — phosphorus 'no atmospheric pool,' eutrophication 'oxygen loss not poison,' and the worked productivity math are all internally consistent and AP-aligned. The only residual is cosmetic: the biomagnification narration presents '10x per step' a touch more rule-like than the 'illustrative' slide label, worth a one-word hedge but not blocking. Verdict: pass.
